headline: Woman's 'I'm Just A Girl' Is Code For 'I Will Use My Gender As An Excuse For Weaponized Incompetence'
summary: 'I can't change a tire, I'm just a girl,' Amanda said. Her mechanic friend taught her in 10 minutes. 'I forgot,' Amanda said.
fullBody: Amanda JustAGirl uses her gender as an excuse for not learning things. 'I can't do math, I'm just a girl,' Amanda said. She can do math. 'I can't parallel park, I'm just a girl,' Amanda said. She can parallel park. Her friends are exhausted. 'It's not cute,' one said. Amanda has started a podcast called 'Just a Girl.' Episode 1: 'Weaponized Incompetence.' The irony is lost on her.
